meta.name: ASRM Altman Self-Rating Mania Scale
meta.description: The ASRM is a brief 5-item self-rating scale used to screen for and assess the severity of manic symptoms.
meta.author: Altman et al. (1997)
meta.reference: Altman EG, et al. The Altman Self-Rating Mania Scale. Biological Psychiatry. 1997;42(10):948-955.
meta.instruction: Please select the option that best describes your state over the past week.

questions.text: I feel happier or more cheerful than usual (not just feeling good)
I feel more self-confident than usual
I need less sleep than usual
I talk more than usual or speak faster
I have been more active or do more things than usual

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, with no indication of significant manic symptoms. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Maintain regular sleep and daily routines
Continue a healthy lifestyle
Monitor your mood periodically with self-assessments if you experience mood fluctuations
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

ranges.label: Mild Mania Tendency
ranges.description: Your score suggests a possible mild manic tendency (a score of 6 or above is the screening cutoff). You may be experiencing elevated mood, increased energy, or reduced need for sleep.
suggestions: Monitor your mood changes and sleep patterns closely
Avoid excessive stimulation and risky behaviors
Maintain a regular daily routine
Consider consulting a psychiatrist for further evaluation
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: Moderate Mania
ranges.description: Your score suggests moderate manic symptoms. You may be experiencing noticeable mood elevation, hyperactivity, and decreased need for sleep. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consult a psychiatrist as soon as possible
Avoid making major decisions or acting impulsively
Inform a trusted family member or friend about your condition
Maintain regular sleep hours and avoid stimulating substances
Consider consulting a mental health professional

ranges.label: Severe Mania
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe manic symptoms, which may include extremely elevated mood, significant sleep reduction, and loss of behavioral control. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Seek immediate professional psychiatric treatment
Have a family member or friend accompany you to a medical appointment
Avoid all major decisions and high-risk behaviors
If currently in treatment, contact your treating physician immediately
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
